req.title: Create encounter (audit)
req.statement: A clinician shall create an Encounter for a patient with start and end timestamps; end before start shall be rejected. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates: (1) successful Encounter creation with valid start < end timestamps, (2) rejection with HTTP 422 when end < start, (3) AuditEvent emission for every operation. I apply EP for the happy path (valid partition), NEG for the invalid timestamp ordering, BVA for the boundary where end equals start (zero-duration encounter), STC to verify the state transition from no-encounter to stored-encounter with audit, and DT for a combined decision covering both timestamp validity and audit presence. Tests are self-contained via fixtures that create and tear down resources deterministically.

tests.name: test_create_encounter_valid_timestamps_emits_audit
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture
Patient exists with known ID via <existing_patient_id> fixture
AuditEvent log is queryable at /AuditEvent endpoint
actions: POST /Encounter with start='2024-06-01T08:00:00Z' and end='2024-06-01T09:00:00Z' using clinician token
Assert HTTP 201 and capture returned encounter ID
expected: Stored start and end timestamps match submitted values
At least one AuditEvent record references the created Encounter ID
trace: R-HLT-081: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
def test_create_encounter_valid_timestamps_emits_audit(clinician_auth_token, existing_patient_id, api_base_url):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Encounter",
        "status": "finished",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{existing_patient_id}"},
        "period": {
            "start": "2024-06-01T08:00:00Z",
            "end": "2024-06-01T09:00:00Z"
        }
    }
    resp = <api_post>(f"{api_base_url}/Encounter", json=payload, token=clinician_auth_token)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "Encounter"
    enc_id = body["id"]
    assert body["period"]["start"] == "2024-06-01T08:00:00Z"
    assert body["period"]["end"] == "2024-06-01T09:00:00Z"

    audit_resp = <api_get>(
        f"{api_base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"Encounter/{enc_id}"},
        token=clinician_auth_token
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    audit_entries = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1
    actions = [e["resource"]["action"] for e in audit_entries]
    assert "C" in actions

    <cleanup_resource>(f"{api_base_url}/Encounter/{enc_id}", token=clinician_auth_token)

tests.name: test_create_encounter_end_before_start_returns_422
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture
Patient exists with known ID via <existing_patient_id> fixture
actions: POST /Encounter with start='2024-06-01T10:00:00Z' and end='2024-06-01T09:00:00Z' (end before start)
Assert error body describes timestamp validation failure
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome or error message referencing period/end or timestamp ordering
No Encounter resource is persisted in the system
tests.executable:
def test_create_encounter_end_before_start_returns_422(clinician_auth_token, existing_patient_id, api_base_url):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Encounter",
        "status": "finished",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{existing_patient_id}"},
        "period": {
            "start": "2024-06-01T10:00:00Z",
            "end": "2024-06-01T09:00:00Z"
        }
    }
    resp = <api_post>(f"{api_base_url}/Encounter", json=payload, token=clinician_auth_token)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert any(kw in error_text for kw in ["period", "end", "start", "timestamp", "invalid"])

    search_resp = <api_get>(
        f"{api_base_url}/Encounter",
        params={"subject": existing_patient_id, "date": "2024-06-01"},
        token=clinician_auth_token
    )
    entries = search_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    bad = [e for e in entries
           if e["resource"].get("period", {}).get("end") == "2024-06-01T09:00:00Z"
           and e["resource"].get("period", {}).get("start") == "2024-06-01T10:00:00Z"]
    assert len(bad) == 0

tests.name: test_create_encounter_end_equals_start_accepted_and_audit_emitted
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture
Patient exists with known ID via <existing_patient_id> fixture
AuditEvent log is queryable at /AuditEvent endpoint
actions: POST /Encounter with start='2024-06-01T08:00:00Z' and end='2024-06-01T08:00:00Z' (zero-duration boundary)
expected: HTTP 201 Created is returned (end == start is the exact boundary; not end < start)
Response body contains the Encounter with matching zero-duration period
At least one AuditEvent references the created Encounter
trace: R-HLT-081: end before start shall be rejected — boundary is end == start (not rejected)
R-HLT-081: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
def test_create_encounter_end_equals_start_accepted_and_audit_emitted(clinician_auth_token, existing_patient_id, api_base_url):
    ts = "2024-06-01T08:00:00Z"
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Encounter",
        "status": "finished",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{existing_patient_id}"},
        "period": {"start": ts, "end": ts}
    }
    resp = <api_post>(f"{api_base_url}/Encounter", json=payload, token=clinician_auth_token)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "Encounter"
    enc_id = body["id"]
    assert body["period"]["start"] == ts
    assert body["period"]["end"] == ts

    audit_resp = <api_get>(
        f"{api_base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"Encounter/{enc_id}"},
        token=clinician_auth_token
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    assert len(audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])) >= 1

    <cleanup_resource>(f"{api_base_url}/Encounter/{enc_id}", token=clinician_auth_token)

tests.name: test_encounter_state_transition_planned_to_finished_audit_captured
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture
Patient exists with known ID via <existing_patient_id> fixture
System supports Encounter status values: 'planned' and 'finished'
actions: POST /Encounter with status='planned' and start='2024-07-01T07:00:00Z' (no end)
Assert HTTP 201 and capture encounter ID
PUT /Encounter/<id> updating status to 'finished' and adding end='2024-07-01T08:00:00Z'
GET /AuditEvent?entity=Encounter/<id> and confirm two audit entries (create + update)
expected: Initial POST returns HTTP 201 with status='planned'
PUT returns HTTP 200 with status='finished' and valid period
AuditEvent log contains at least two entries: one for 'C' (create) and one for 'U' (update)
No 422 is raised since end > start in the final state
trace: R-HLT-081: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
R-HLT-081: clinician opens encounter (trigger maps to status transition)

tests.name: test_encounter_decision_table_timestamp_and_audit_combinations
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture
Patient exists with known ID via <existing_patient_id> fixture
AuditEvent log is queryable at /AuditEvent endpoint
actions: Case A: POST with end > start — expect 201 and audit entry present
Case B: POST with end < start — expect 422 and no persisted resource
Case C: POST with missing end — expect 201 (open encounter) and audit entry present
expected: Case C: 201 + AuditEvent action='C' with no end timestamp in period
trace: R-HLT-081: end before start shall be rejected
R-HLT-081: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_encounter_decision_table_timestamp_and_audit_combinations(clinician_auth_token, existing_patient_id, api_base_url):
    created_ids = []

    # Case A: valid end > start
    payload_a = {
        "resourceType": "Encounter", "status": "finished",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{existing_patient_id}"},
        "period": {"start": "2024-08-01T08:00:00Z", "end": "2024-08-01T09:00:00Z"}
    }
    resp_a = <api_post>(f"{api_base_url}/Encounter", json=payload_a, token=clinician_auth_token)
    assert resp_a.status_code == 201
    id_a = resp_a.json()["id"]
    created_ids.append(id_a)
    audit_a = <api_get>(f"{api_base_url}/AuditEvent", params={"entity": f"Encounter/{id_a}"}, token=clinician_auth_token)
    assert any(e["resource"]["action"] == "C" for e in audit_a.json().get("entry", []))

    # Case B: invalid end < start
    payload_b = {
        "resourceType": "Encounter", "status": "finished",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{existing_patient_id}"},
        "period": {"start": "2024-08-01T10:00:00Z", "end": "2024-08-01T09:00:00Z"}
    }
    resp_b = <api_post>(f"{api_base_url}/Encounter", json=payload_b, token=clinician_auth_token)
    assert resp_b.status_code == 422

    # Case C: missing end (open encounter)
    payload_c = {
        "resourceType": "Encounter", "status": "in-progress",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{existing_patient_id}"},
        "period": {"start": "2024-08-01T11:00:00Z"}
    }
    resp_c = <api_post>(f"{api_base_url}/Encounter", json=payload_c, token=clinician_auth_token)
    assert resp_c.status_code == 201
    id_c = resp_c.json()["id"]
    created_ids.append(id_c)
    assert "end" not in resp_c.json().get("period", {})
    audit_c = <api_get>(f"{api_base_url}/AuditEvent", params={"entity": f"Encounter/{id_c}"}, token=clinician_auth_token)
    assert any(e["resource"]["action"] == "C" for e in audit_c.json().get("entry", []))

    for eid in created_ids:
        <cleanup_resource>(f"{api_base_url}/Encounter/{eid}", token=clinician_auth_token)
